derivations.title: Motional Electromotive Force (Motional EMF)
derivations.priority_reason: Fundamental concept linking mechanics and electromagnetism. Basis for railgun problems and sliding rod numericals.
derivations.diagram_description: A U-shaped conducting rail with a movable rod PQ of length l resting on it. The rod moves to the left with velocity v. Uniform magnetic field B acts perpendicular to the plane (into the page, represented by crosses). The position of the rod is at distance x from the left end.

given_assumptions: Uniform magnetic field $B$ perpendicular to the plane.
Rod PQ of length $l$ moving with velocity $v$.
Distance of rod from reference is $x$.

steps.text: Magnetic flux enclosed by the loop PQRS at instant $t$:
By Faraday's Law of Induction, induced emf is the rate of change of flux:
Since $B$ and $l$ are constant, pull them out. Velocity $v = -dx/dt$ (negative because $x$ decreases as rod moves left):

final_result.text: The magnitude of motional emf is:

examiner_notes: Sign convention: explicitly state why v = -dx/dt if reducing area.
Lorentz force method (F=qvB => W=F*l => emf=W/q) is also accepted but Faraday's law is preferred for this specific phrasing.
Must mention B, l, and v are mutually perpendicular.
question_forms: Derive an expression for the motional emf induced in a conductor moving in a uniform magnetic field.
A rod of length l moves with velocity v in a magnetic field B. Find the induced emf.

derivations.title: Mutual Inductance of Two Long Coaxial Solenoids
derivations.priority_reason: Most frequent 3 or 5 mark derivation in this chapter. Often asks to prove M12 = M21.
derivations.diagram_description: Two long coaxial solenoids. Inner solenoid S1 (radius r1, turns/length n1). Outer solenoid S2 (radius r2, turns/length n2). Length of both is l. Current I2 flows in S2 creating field B2.

given_assumptions: Two long coaxial solenoids of length $l$.

steps.text: Magnetic field produced by current $I_2$ in outer solenoid $S_2$ (uniform inside):
Total magnetic flux through inner solenoid $S_1$. Note: Flux exists only over area $A_1 = \pi r_1^2$.
By definition, $\phi_1 = M_{12} I_2$. Equating the two expressions:

examiner_notes: Crucial Point: When calculating flux through outer solenoid due to inner current, the field is non-zero ONLY inside the inner radius r1. Thus area is always pi*r1^2.
Reciprocity: Students must state that M12 = M21.
question_forms: Derive the expression for the mutual inductance of two long coaxial solenoids.
Show that the mutual inductance between two coils depends on their geometry and number of turns.

derivations.title: Self-Inductance of a Long Solenoid
derivations.priority_reason: Standard derivation, often serves as a backup to Mutual Inductance.
derivations.diagram_description: A long solenoid of length l, area A, carrying current I. Magnetic field lines shown parallel inside.

given_assumptions: Long solenoid of length $l$, area $A$, total turns $N$.

steps.text: Total magnetic flux linked with the solenoid ($N$ turns):
By definition $\phi = LI$. Comparing the terms:

final_result.text: In terms of turn density $n = N/l$:

examiner_notes: Confusion between N (total turns) and n (turns per unit length).
Formula dependence: L is proportional to N squared.
question_forms: Obtain the expression for the self-inductance of a long solenoid.
On what factors does the self-inductance of a solenoid depend?

derivations.title: Energy Stored in an Inductor (Magnetic Potential Energy)
derivations.priority_reason: Analogue to capacitor energy. Frequently asked.
derivations.diagram_description: Simple circuit with Inductor L connected to a battery and key.

steps.text: Induced emf opposes the growth of current: $\varepsilon = -L \frac{dI}{dt}$.
Work done by external source to maintain current $I$ against this emf in time $dt$:
steps.latex: dW = |\varepsilon| I dt = \left( L \frac{dI}{dt} \right) I dt = LI dI

steps.text: Total work done to build current from $0$ to $I$:

examiner_notes: Must mention that work is done against the back-emf.
Integration limits (0 to I) must be shown.
question_forms: Derive an expression for the energy stored in an inductor.
Prove that magnetic energy density is B^2/2mu0 (requires substituting L and B formulas).

derivations.title: AC Generator: Induced EMF in a Rotating Coil
derivations.priority_reason: High-priority 5-mark question involving diagram, principle, and derivation.
derivations.diagram_description: Rectangular coil PQRS mounted between curved magnetic poles N and S. Coil rotates with angular velocity omega about an axis perpendicular to B. Slip rings and brushes collect current. Angle theta is between Area vector A and Magnetic field B.

given_assumptions: Coil of $N$ turns, Area $A$ rotating with angular velocity $\omega$.

steps.text: At time $t$, angle between area vector $\vec{A}$ and field $\vec{B}$ is $\theta = \omega t$.
By Faraday's law, induced emf is:

final_result.text: Instantaneous emf (where $\varepsilon_0 = NBA\omega$ is peak emf):

examiner_notes: Angle definition is critical: Theta is between Normal to coil and B. If plane of coil is used, formula becomes cos.
Chain rule of differentiation: Students often forget to multiply by omega.
Graph of emf vs time (sine wave) is usually required for the 5th mark.
question_forms: Describe the principle and construction of an AC generator. Derive the expression for instantaneous emf.
Show that the emf induced in a rotating coil varies sinusoidally with time.
